summary: Ukrainian FPV pilots are targeting dismounted Russian infantry, including attempts to dismount troops from horses before strikes. Russian forces are deploying air defense drones like "Yolka" for kinetic intercepts of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, while the "Rubikon" Center reported 104 strikes on Ukrainian personnel across multiple directions on March 11, 2026. A Russian soldier was killed in a bayonet charge against a Ukrainian FPV drone, and another was hit by two simultaneous FPV strikes while taking cover behind a tree. Ukrainian forces are fielding advanced FPV models with complex mounts, prompting Russian countermeasures, while Russian electronic warfare struggles to counter "Molniya" drones due to unknown control frequencies. Additionally, a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle was destroyed by a Russian fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" in Dobropole.
